Storage Formats

Storage Formats

DBMS vendors use the term data type to describe the way data is handled and stored on disk, but this invariably means the way the data is actually stored. In Uniface documentation, the term storage format is therefore used to refer to DBMS data types . A Uniface data type is specified for each field in a modeled entity; a DBMS storage format is internal to the DBMS.

For example, the Sybase Datetime data type is referred to as the Sybase Datetime storage format.

Uniface maps the Uniface data type to the DBMS storage format using a packing code, which is specified in the field's Database Interface property, also known as the field interface. In most cases, a field interface definition consists of only a packing code (and optional field length), but it can also contain rules for sub-fields and variable-length techniques.

The DBMS connector maps the field interface definition to the most appropriate DBMS storage format. By determining the optimum storage format in the DBMS connector rather than in the modeled entity, applications can remain independent of the underlying DBMS.

Type of DBMS

Mapping a Uniface data type and packing code to a storage format depends on the DBMS, and whether it is a field-level or record-level database:

  • Field-level DBMSs generally have their own packing codes for Boolean, Numeric, Float, Date, Time, and DateTime. The Uniface logical packing codes specified in the field interface are taken into consideration when storing data, but only if they do not conflict with those prescribed by the underlying DBMS.
  • Record-level DBMSs are not concerned with fields, and do not have packing codes. They usually reserve sufficient room in the record to accommodate storage of the data using your specified data types and packing codes.

From Model Definitions To Storage Formats

The Uniface data type and the packing code defined in the application model are used at runtime to determine the storage format and layout, as shown in the following table:

Table 1. Conversion Process From Modeled Entity to Storage Format
Application Model Runtime
Definition Property
Entity Database Interface Uniface uses this information to identify the DBMS connector to use and to obtain logon information from the assignment file.

In an assignment file, this path can be assigned to another path (for example, $ORA=$MSS or $DEF=$SYB).

Field

 Data Type Uniface knows what kind of data to expect in this field and how to perform data validation.
Database Interface—a packing code is entered for the data in a field in the field interface definition. If no packing code is specified, Uniface supplies a default based on the data type. Uniface knows how to store the data in the DBMS, and what the preferred storage layout in the DBMS is.
Related Links
  • Packing Codes

    Packing codes are mappings between Uniface data types and DBMS data types (storage formats). When writing a field, Uniface passes the data and a valid packing code to the DBMS connector, which uses the packing code to determine the appropriate storage format for storing the data in the target DBMS. Packing codes also determine how data is interfaced with 3GL routines.
